reforms.description: The US government has significantly increased its involvement in active manpower programmes, focusing on skill training, work experience, and job placement assistance for disadvantaged groups, particularly youths and minorities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of disadvantaged groups, these programmes aim to improve employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Outlays for manpower programmes have grown significantly, indicating active government involvement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a substantial shift in government policy towards active labour market interventions, addressing critical issues of unemployment and skill shortages in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The US government became substantially involved in active manpower programmes in the 1960's. Programmes include skill training, work experience, job placement assistance, and social and health services.
